item.title: Logan sketches path to shrink size of Fed balance sheet
item.description: Dallas Federal Reserve President Lorie Logan on Thursday laid out paths and options for the U.S. central bank вҖӢto reduce the size of its balance sheet, in remarks that noted the current system works well and offers benefits for вҖҢoverall financial stability.

item.title: Starbucks expands weekly pay, adds more tips and bonuses for US baristas
item.description: Starbucks will move to weekly pay for all U.S. store workers and roll out a program вҖӢthat adds bonuses and expands tips, it said on вҖҢThursday, as concerns persist over its stalled talks with the union representing some U.S. baristas.

item.title: Exclusive: Russia's Primorsk oil terminal lost 40% of storage to drone attacks, satellite images show
item.description: Russia's Baltic Sea port of Primorsk, вҖҢone of country's largest export gateways, lost at least 40% of its storage facilities in Ukrainian drone attacks last month, U.S. commercial satellite images seen by Reuters showed on Thursday.

item.title: McDonald's launches under $3 menu items and $4 breakfast deals in US
item.description: McDonald's said on Thursday it was introducing menu items priced at $3 or less and offering вҖӢa $4 breakfast meal deal in the U.S., вҖҢas restaurants aim to attract price-conscious diners worn down by economic uncertainties.

item.title: US trade deficit widens in February as imports offset record exports
item.description: The U.S. trade deficit widened in February as a rebound in imports offset strong growth in exports, which increased to a record high, potentially keeping trade on track to subtract from economic growth in the вҖӢfirst quarter.

item.title: US asset manager shares drop after Blue Owl limits withdrawals in two funds
item.description: Shares of U.S. asset management giants fell before the вҖӢopening bell on Thursday after вҖҢBlue Owl capped the amount investors can withdraw from two of вҖӢits retail-focused funds, stoking вҖӢfresh concerns about the industry.

item.title: Ford's first-quarter US auto sales fall amid affordability concerns
item.description: Ford вҖӢMotor reported вҖҢa вҖӢnear вҖӢ9% drop вҒ in вҖӢfirst-quarter вҖӢU.S. sales on вҖӢThursday, вҖӢas the вҖҢbroader вҒ industry navigates вҖӢongoing вҖӢaffordability вҒ concerns.

item.title: BP names Carol Howle as deputy CEO, to oversee portfolio review, strategy
item.description: BP named Carol Howle as deputy chief вҖӢexecutive on Thursday and said she would oversee the вҖҢcompany's ongoing portfolio review and strategy development.

item.title: Tesla's first-quarter deliveries miss estimates as tax credit expiry weighs
item.description: Tesla missed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter deliveries on Thursday, as the expiry of U.S. tax credits on the purchase of electric vehicles weighed on demand, sending вҖӢits shares down nearly 4% in premarket trading.

item.title: KKR raises $23 billion for North America private equity fund
item.description: Investment firm KKR said on Thursday it had raised $23 billion for вҖӢits North America-focused private equity fund, вҖҢthe largest such fund the firm has devoted to the region.

item.title: Blue Owl limits withdrawals in two funds after historic surge in redemption requests
item.description: Blue Owl is limiting withdrawals from two of its вҖӢfunds after a historic level of redemption requests came in вҖҢfor the first quarter, the company told investors Thursday, as AI-related worries drove an investor exodus from its technology-focused fund.

item.title: US defense stocks see no Iran war lift after early surge
item.description: U.S. defense stocks have declined even as the Iran war drags on, indicating that the typical "buy-on-conflict" trade had largely peaked in the вҖӢweeks before in anticipation of tougher action by President Donald Trump.

item.title: US weekly jobless claims fall as layoffs remain low
item.description: New applications for U.S. unemployment benefits fell last week amid low layoffs, suggesting labor market conditions remained calm in March, though economists have warned that a prolonged war in the Middle вҖӢEast posed a downside risk.

item.title: Rivian beats quarterly delivery estimates as EV demand stabilizes
item.description: Rivian Automotive exceeded analysts' expectations for first-quarter vehicle deliveries, indicating stabilized demand for its SUVs and R1T pickup trucks вҖӢafter a decline last year due to the end вҖҢof U.S. tax credits for electric vehicles.

item.title: Global companies delay IPOs and slash dividends as Middle East conflict rattles markets
item.description: The Middle East conflict has significantly impacted global financial markets, affected logistics and hindered the supply of raw materials integral вҖӢto a host of industries.

item.title: China expands digital yuan programme with 12 new bank operators
item.description: China's central bank has expanded its digital yuan programme by adding a вҖӢdozen additional banks as operators, confirming a Reuters report вҖҢlast month.

item.title: Wall Street sees multi-billion dollar debut for Lilly's obesity pill despite Novo lead
item.description: Eli Lilly's newly approved weight-loss pill is expected to generate billions in sales as soon as this year, with analysts betting it will quickly вҖӢnarrow Novo Nordisk's head start in the oral market.

item.title: Exclusive: Russian oil output cuts are unavoidable as drone attacks shrink exports
item.description: Russian oil output cuts are imminent because Ukraine's strikes on port infrastructure, pipelines and refineries have reduced export capability by 1 million barrels per day, or a вҖӢfifth of total capacity, three industry sources said on Thursday.

item.title: Morning Bid: No Easter truce
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Goldman Sachs completes Innovator Capital acquisition, lifting ETF assets to $90 billion
item.description: Goldman Sachs said on Thursday it has completed the acquisition of active exchange-traded fund provider вҖӢInnovator Capital Management, expanding the Wall Street вҖҢbank's presence in the fast-growing active ETF segment.

item.title: Tariff-struck companies exploring loans backed by refund claimsвҖӢ
item.description: Some tariff-whiplashed companies are exploring using refund claims as collateral for loans, in the latest example of creative financing arising from the complicated process вҖӢof getting refunds from Donald Trump's now-overturned "Liberation Day" tariffs.

item.title: Focus: Netflix searches for franchises after losing out on Harry Potter
item.description: After losing its bid for Warner Bros DiscoveryвҖҷs rich trove of characters and stories, Netflix is forging ahead with the challenging work of building culture-defining franchises on its own.

item.title: Tesla's China-made EV sales rise for second straight quarter
item.description: Tesla's China-made electric vehicle sales rose for a second consecutive quarter, holding up despite вҖӢintensifying competition as the U.S. automaker broadened its вҖҢfocus beyond EVs.

item.title: Ahead of Greek social media ban, parents desperate to separate children from phones
item.description: Greek mother Georgia вҖӢEfstathiou has tried everything to loosen the grip that social media has on her 14-year-old son: heart-to-heart talks; вҖҢinternet-free time; confiscating his phone. Arguments flare as she fights the allure of his screen and its videos and messages.

item.title: France to announce measures to favour data centres in coming weeks, Finance Minister says
item.description: French Finance вҖӢMinister Roland вҖҢLescure said on Thursday вҖӢhis вҖӢgovernment expects to вҒ announce in вҖӢthe coming вҖӢweeks new measures to encourage вҖӢsetting вҖӢup data centres in вҖҢthe вҒ country.

item.title: Wall St futures slide as Trump signals tougher Iran strikes, oil jumps 6%
item.description: Futures tracking Wall Street's main indexes slid вҖӢon Thursday as U.S. President Donald Trump signaled more aggressive attacks on Iran, dampening expectations for an вҖҢend to the month-long war and sending crude prices up 6%.

item.title: China says it supports law-abiding transnational deals after reports of Meta deal review
item.description: The Chinese government supports companies вҖӢwith transnational operations вҖҢand technology cooperation deals according to needs and вҖӢthe law, вҖӢcommerce ministry spokesperson He Yadong вҒ said on Thursday.

item.title: Trump's fresh Iran threats give investors a risk-off reality check
item.description: President Donald Trump's threat to bomb Iran back to the Stone Age has sharply raised stakes in a war now вҖӢin its fifth week and crushed investors' hopes for a swift end to a conflict that is squeezing oil supplies and fanning inflation.

item.title: Global central banks mostly on hold as war muddies economic outlook
item.description: Major central banks pointed to uncertainty linked to the war in the Middle East вҖҢas they largely kept interest rates steady in March, with concerns over higher inflation and weaker growth clouding the global economic outlook.

item.title: Central banks' inflation mood puzzle: more judgment than science
item.description: The world's central bankers may be attempting the impossible: to get into the psyche of business executives, labour unions and ordinary households in real time to understand how they are navigating their finances вҖӢthrough yet another energy shock.
